author: BREND
desc: Systems thinking: foundations, dynamic patterns, governing rules, tools, leadership, people factors.

defs.d: Circular causal chain; output feeds back as input. Reinforcing or balancing.
Demarcation of what is inside/outside the system of interest.
Accumulations (stocks) changed by rates (flows); fundamental system structure.
Balancing loop with delay produces cycles around goal.
Load exceeds capacity with delayed feedback; decline after lag.
Driving variable exceeds threshold; abrupt state transition.
Growth rate follows logistic curve; approaches resource constraint.
Demand signal variance amplifies monotonically upstream.
Capacity to perceive, use, understand, and manage emotions.
Novel recombination within constraints; divergent then convergent.
Style adapts to follower readiness; directing → coaching → supporting → delegating.
Leader serves followers' needs first; authority derives from service.
Marginal gain decreases as input increases past inflection.

thms.stmt: demand signal variance → amplification increases monotonically upstream
growth rate → logistic-like curve, → 0 as stock → R (resource constraint)
balancing loop + delay → controlled variable oscillates around goal
load > capacity + delayed feedback → decline after lag ∝ delay
driving variable > θ → abrupt state transition
Variety(R) ≥ Variety(S) required for control; under-variety → uncontrolled disturbances
M adopted as target T → agents optimize M directly, breaking M↔G causal link

xlinks.note: feedback loop structure ↔ ordered story steps
Bayesian updating → evidence accumulation toward tipping θ
system boundary ↔ geometric scope / homothety scaling

prov.author: BREND
prov.basis: Consolidated from SYSTEMS-CORE v2.0. Densified v2.1: stripped training-weight defs/facts, kept thm IDs, all xlinks preserved. Restored v2.2: 14 minimal defs re-added to support xlinks from PERSONALITY, BUILDSCI, LOGIC, STATS.

src_map.SYS.REF: MIT OCW 15.988, 'System Dynamics Self Study' (Forrester). https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/15-988-system-dynamics-self-study-fall-1998-spring-1999/ — feedback loops, stocks/flows, system dynamics | Canavesi & Minelli (2021), 'Servant Leadership: Systematic Literature Review', Empl Responsib Rights J 34(3):267-289, DOI:10.1007/s10672-021-09381-3 — leadership models
